intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent or entirely obscured. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens; the pots appear to contain only soil and debris. P3 (Pothos) is present but severely diminished, represented only by a single, struggling stem.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty soil surface observed throughout the sequence.
plant_audit.P2_Black_Pot: No foliage detected; soil surface appears barren.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot: Single green stem remains; shows signs of etiolation and lack of vigor.
plant_audit.P4_Shared_Black_Pot: No distinct specimen identified; appears to be part of the empty soil mass.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp-appearing, with no visible signs of active root growth or secondary seedlings.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of crushed eggshells (calcium amendment) on the surface of the P3 pot.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant organic matter or fungal blooms detected.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: Minimal change; the single stem in P3 remains static in its posture.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: No observable growth or decay; the biome is in a state of stasis/dormancy.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The lack of foliage in P1, P2, and P4 suggests total loss. P3 is the only survivor, but its singular stem indicates a high probability of imminent mortality due to lack of photosynthetic surface area.
anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments as a soil amendment.
Complete absence of expected plant mass in 75% of the registry.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the botanical collection. The environment appears to be a 'ghost biome' where only one remnant of a Pothos (P3) persists. The soil in all pots is devoid of life, and the lack of change over the 5-day observation window suggests the specimens are either dead or in a state of terminal decline. The intervention of eggshells suggests a previous attempt at soil enrichment that failed to support the plants.
